publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-11-17 23:11:39 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Minutes, correspondence, financial records, speeches and publications. Operational files, beginning with the papers for the Committee on the Regional Plan of New York of the Russell Sage Foundation and the papers of Charles D. Norton. Material from the New York Metropolitan Region Study done by Harvard and the Regional Plan Association (1956-1961); study of migration in industry by Henry Fagin (1953-1954), Arden House conferences; Inter-County Planning Conference; Race for Open Space, working notes and folders; American Institute of Planners committee reports, minutes, and correspondence; county reference files for the area served by RPA; American Planning and Civic Association, minutes, reports, and correspondence; information files on all subjects connected with urban life; and scrapbooks of clippings about the Regional Plan Association. Major correspondents include Thomas Adams, Frederic Delano, Richard Hurd, Lawson Purdy, Lewis Mumford, Richard Whitten, Edward Bassett, Lawrence Orton, and Flavel Shurtleff.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Regional Plan Association records, #2688. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Regional planning.
City planning.
City and town life.
Business relocation.
controlaccess.corpname: Russell Sage Foundation. Committee on the Regional Plan of New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

p.extref: A guide to the glass slides is available by clicking here. (Searchable PDF)

scopecontent.p: The glass slides are believed to be in boxes 123-132, 137, 242-263

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Removed from box 4, folder 16 and flattened.

Looseleaf Scrapbooks of clippings, reviews, orders, etc. on "Planning & Community Appearance", report by Joint Committee of N.Y. Chapter of American of lanners, in cooperation with Regional Plan Association. Henry Fagin & Robert C. Weinberg

Scrapbooks of clippings on Open Space Project publications. BK 1 "Preserving Open Space" Indexed for Law of Open Space, Dynamics of Park Demand, Nature in Metropolis. BK 2 Indexed for Race for Open Space, Williams Bill, Municipal Art Society, East Hampton Star, Island Beach.
Case History of a Regional Plan recommendation, binder (traced in the newspaper clippings)

processinfo.p: Part of series in Box 124. The glass lantern slide was broken and a film negative file & broken slide discarded (9/1996)
